Source_Species.reason: The paper studies the iad locus in Variovorax paradoxus CL14, so the source genus Variovorax is supported.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The Results explicitly refer to the locus in Variovorax paradoxus CL14.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: CL14 is the strain used throughout the Results for the Variovorax iad locus.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Complementation with iadDE versus iadCDE, knockout of iadCDE, IAA degradation assays, and Arabidopsis root assays are reported. However, there was no iadC-only test and no specific iadC single deletion; evidence is indirect via iadCDE comparison.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Fig. 1d,e and text show iadDE alone degrades IAA more slowly than iadCDE and only iadCDE restores Arthrobacter CL28-dependent RGI to wild-type levels.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported in aggregate: iadC is not sufficient alone/not shown alone but only acts with iadDE in the reported constructs; iadCDE improves degradation/rescue versus iadDE; and iadC is annotated as a ferredoxin subunit in Fig. 1 legend, consistent with electron transfer support.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Fig. 1 legend states genes are identified by last two digits of IMG gene ID 26436136##, and gene 70 is iadC.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied strain in the experiments is Variovorax paradoxus CL14.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: IAA is explicitly identified as an auxin/plant hormone.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: iadC is co-functional with iadD and iadE in the same module and annotated as a ferredoxin subunit, but direct physical interaction between IadC and IadD/IadE was not experimentally shown.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: RNA-seq shows the locus is upregulated in the presence of IAA; iadC is part of that locus.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: CL14 is the strain used for the applied experiments.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The metabolite measured and degraded is indole-3-acetic acid (IAA).

Other_Important_Info.reason: Fig. 1 legend annotates iadC as a ferredoxin subunit, and the Discussion states other ferredoxin subunits in the genome may compensate when iadC is absent.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: IAA was measured in bacterial culture supernatant and plant root phenotypes were assessed in Arabidopsis assays, but the paper does not explicitly state 'IAA accumulation site' as rhizosphere/root environment.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The relevant experiments used IAA supplementation and duo-association with Arthrobacter CL28.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq data are reported and GEO accession GSE210968 is listed in Data availability.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking interpretation. The paper suggests utility for developing strains with auxin signal interference properties, but does not specifically frame iadC inclusion in engineered strains in these terms.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: iadC is annotated as a ferredoxin subunit in the figure legend and Discussion.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results show iadC increases degradation rate and is required, together with iadDE, for full CL28-RGI rescue.
